source: user-agent feedback loop on CodeWiki compiler architecture

decisions.summary: Use .codewiki as the single project-local root for CodeWiki-owned state.
Use .codewiki/kb as the canonical knowledge base; do not keep durable knowledge under .wiki.
Use three compilers: feedback, documentation, and implementation. Each compiler produces a build artifact for the layer below.
Compiler outputs are feedback_build, documentation_build, and implementation_build. They are agent-first, token-efficient artifacts, not prose archives.
Validation gateways sit at handoffs and judge horizontal and vertical alignment. Use validation gateway as the preferred term; verifier remains an implementation role where needed.
Feedback compiler may read current .codewiki/kb for grounding, but writes only after the feedback_build is accepted.
Documentation compiler updates .codewiki/kb and roadmap/task packs from an accepted feedback_build.
Implementation compiler executes task packs with TDD. Tests live in code/test directories, not inside knowledge artifacts.
Implementation compiler may split into tester and builder roles: tester derives tests from task pack, builder makes them pass.
Use a graph-first generated read model: .codewiki/index_graph.json. Avoid a broad view zoo; derive any status/queue lenses from the graph when needed.
Persist failed, blocked, or policy-required validation reports. Passing validation need not be stored by default.
Do not store raw events by default; rely on git and agent harness session storage for full history.
Feedback compiler is not a separate outer loop; it is the escalation path any compiler uses when ambiguity or unmapped intent appears.
Keep temporary .wiki/config.json symlink only as compatibility bridge while host tools still resolve .wiki.

target_structure..codewiki/validation/**: failed, blocked, or policy-kept validation reports

horizontal: kb docs agree with kb docs
roadmap tasks agree with roadmap tasks
code components agree with code components

open_implementation_delta: Refactor current graph/view code to generate .codewiki/index_graph.json as the primary view.
Replace old view-audit terminology with graph/index validation.
Add first-class build artifact schemas and validation gateway profiles.
Update package skills so router + three compiler workflow docs match this model.
